visual_attributes.morphology: thickset body with relatively short, robust limbs
distinctive_features: row of spines extending from the nape along the back to the tail
conical and pointed scales on the top of the head
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: spines are more elaborate on Fernandina Island

domain_specific_visual.sexual_dimorphism: males have longer spines and larger bony plates on the head

functional_attributes.primary_function: forages in the sea for algae

domain_specific_functional.breeding_strategy: females dig nest holes in soil for egg laying
domain_specific_functional.thermoregulation: dark tones allow rapid heat absorption

relationships.description: Marine iguanas are found only on the Galápagos Islands.
Marine iguanas and Galapagos land iguanas evolved from a common ancestor.
Rare hybridization occurs where their ranges overlap.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: diverged from land iguanas 4.5 million years ago
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: considered unique among modern lizards for marine foraging
